recordings.title: Six deaths from bad-state clicks
recordings.feedbackTitle: Six deaths from bad-state clicks
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you kept looking for farm and side value after the map was already collapsing, so the enemy got to trade your jungle time into base pressure and another death. Fix: at the mistake timestamp, drop the camp or side wave and move to the closest base-defense line until teammates are alive.
recordings.gameDetail: At 23:33, in a 1/6/1, 152 CS, 25-minute ranked game, you are taking a side jungle camp while enemies are pressuring through your side of the map; your job is to leave the camp, walk toward the inhibitor/base line, and clear only the wave that reaches turret instead of spending more seconds on camp gold. At 23:33 the feeding pattern is not that you never farm; it is that normal farm stays on the menu after the map state already says defend. At 25:30 the consequence is visible: you are dead, an allied turret has fallen, and enemies are inside the base area because every extra second on the camp left Samira away from the only place that could stop the push. At 17:42, the better shape briefly appears because you are mid with an ally and a wave; mid is better in that state because it is the shortest route to defend or hit structure and your ally can stand between Samira and a collapse. At 19:39 you are again near river with an ally, which is useful only if it turns into wave control, vision, or a safe fight. When you are behind or already dying a lot, one safe defensive wave is worth more than one jungle camp.
recordings.secondaryFocus: Second focus: camera and map checks - next game, before taking a camp after 15 minutes, glance at mid wave, death timers, and nearest threatened turret; if any are bad, leave the camp.
recordings.eventEvidence: 17:42 shows Samira mid with an ally and a wave; 19:39 shows Samira near river with an ally; 23:33 shows Samira alone on a side jungle camp while the enemy map state is pushing in; 25:30 shows Samira dead as an allied turret falls and enemies are in the base area.
recordings.failureEvidence: Failure evidence: at 23:33 Samira chooses a side jungle camp while the visible map state is already about base defense, so the failure is not farming in general; it is farming after the defend signal. By 25:30 the cost is visible because Samira is dead, an allied turret has fallen, and enemies are inside the base area.
recordings.goodThing: At 17:42 you did find the correct map lane with an ally and a mid wave; that is the shape to repeat before it turns into side-camp drift.
recordings.whyTrust: The review is tied to the visible collapse frames and the scoreboard line: 1/6/1 with 152 CS, side-camp choice at 23:33, and death/base consequence at 25:30.
recordings.evidence: Manual frame review of the latest ranked recording plus scoreboard context: 1/6/1, 152 CS, mid-wave setup at 17:42, river hover at 19:39, isolated side camp at 23:33, and death/base collapse at 25:30.
recordings.pattern: The repeated mistake is not mechanics; it is treating a losing map as if normal farming still has equal value. The six deaths matter more than the extra camp, so the first climb target is leaving low-value farm before it becomes another death window.
recordings.diamondRule: When the enemy can hit your base, defensive wave first, jungle camp second.
recordings.drill: Next game, whenever an outer or inhibitor-side turret is threatened, say wave before camp and path to the structure line first.

recordings.title: No conversion from mid lane priority — tempo and conversion discipline leak
recordings.feedbackTitle: No conversion from mid lane priority — tempo and conversion discipline leak
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You repeatedly stayed mid to clear waves without converting lane priority into side/objective threat, collapsing sidelane play, or applying pressure elsewhere while your team was respawning and the map was open. Fix: At 16:05, after clearing the mid wave, rotate to help pressure bot or contest vision/objectives instead of autopiloting another mid wave; if an enemy is clearly showing alone bot, pressure with your team there, if not, use your wave to invade or establish vision for the next Dragon/Baron.
recordings.gameDetail: At 16:05, after clearning the mid wave, Samira remains in midlane with zero enemy pressure and the map in a neutral or losing state, clearly visible from the minimap; rather than resetting, grouping with a sidelane push, or contesting a key objective as her teammates respawn, she idles in mid and takes another wave, gaining minimal value while losing potential payout from side pressure, vision contest, or tempo setup. This sequence gives the enemy time to reset/map control and denies the chance to help teammates with numbers or catch future plays out of fog. The correct action is to immediately evaluate the lowest pressure side, rotate with the next wave, or help start vision contest on the upcoming Dragon, Baron, or collapse a side play, not auto-pilot-farming the next mid wave; every mid wave after you are caught up uncontested is value lost if it doesn't convert to something on the map. This matters because in games with higher drag/baron/side threat, not converting mid priority to side/objective pressure will stall your climb and let the enemy dictate every important map state.
recordings.secondaryFocus: Second focus: Repeatedly clearing mid waves with no camera or attention on sidelane skirmishes—next game, deploy your camera to side lanes after every mid-push even if you aren't rotating, so you spot plays before they're over (mechanics judgment limited by FPS, focus on camera/control and side plays).
recordings.eventEvidence: 9:46 Samira auto-attacking cannon minion at mid turret; 16:05 Samira is mid alone, no enemy contest or structure available, with allied vision and skirmishes breaking out on side lanes per the minimap, but she stays mid to farm. 21:49 Samira running from fountain into base after respawn
recordings.goodThing: Solid wave control and no random mid solo deaths, showing discipline in not pushing for turret chip when unsafe.
recordings.whyTrust: This feedback is grounded in the strategic structure of Samira mid-games, where conversion of wave prio into side/objective threat leads to faster climbs and helps avoid games decided by enemy grouping or side picks.
recordings.evidence: 9:46 Samira auto-attacking cannon minion at mid turret; 16:05 Samira is mid alone, no enemy contest or structure available, with allied vision and skirmishes breaking out on side lanes per the minimap, but she stays mid to farm. 21:49 Samira running from fountain into base after respawn
recordings.pattern: You consistently held mid wave control safely but defaulted to extra mid wave clear instead of scanning or rotating to punish open map/side opportunities after pushes, especially with Dragon/Baron/side showing on minimap.
recordings.diamondRule: If you have mid priority and there is no play to make mid, immediately check for a side rotation or contest vision/next objective instead of defaulting to another mid wave.
recordings.drill: After every uncontested mid wave, instantly move your camera to each side lane and jungle fog—decide if you should rotate or contest for vision/objective, and do not let yourself autopilot back to mid.

recordings.title: Losing fights need a next-click script
recordings.feedbackTitle: Losing fights need a next-click script
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you kept playing messy or losing states as if one more fight would fix them, even when enemies were alive, your health was dropping, or teammates were dead. Fix: at the mistake timestamp, back out, clear only the safe wave, or wait behind the first ally before entering again.
recordings.gameDetail: At 23:33 the mid push has turned into a crowded fight with three enemy bodies still in front of you and an enemy turret/base line behind them; your job is to stand behind the first ally, hit the nearest safe target or wave, and leave as soon as your health drops instead of staying available for the full re-engage. By 23:43 you are dead with multiple allies also down, so the leak is not that you lacked damage; the leak is that the fight continued after the safe damage window ended and gave the enemy a clean push back. At 24:20, when you respawn with several teammates still dead, do not run out looking for a fight or a side route; hold base, clear the closest wave under turret, and wait for at least two teammates to spawn. At 26:54 the same rule appears again with four allies dead: stop at the base/inhibitor line, do not walk up bottom jungle or lane, and only clear minions that enter tower range. The simple next-game script is fight from behind a body while the state is even, and when the state is losing, defend one safe wave until teammates are alive.
recordings.secondaryFocus: Second focus: lane and fight spacing - next game, do not cross past your support/frontline until the enemy engage tool is used; at 4:55 and 7:16 the safer habit is last-hit from Q range and let the wave come to you.
recordings.eventEvidence: 23:33 shows Samira behind a Baron-powered mid push with multiple enemy defenders still alive; 23:43 shows Samira dead after the fight continues; 24:20 shows Samira respawning while several teammates are still dead; 26:54 shows Samira moving out while four allies are dead.
recordings.failureEvidence: Failure evidence: at 23:33 the fight is already crowded with enemy defenders alive, so the safe damage window is ending. By 23:43 Samira is dead with allies also down; at 24:20 and 26:54 the visible failure repeats as forward movement starts while teammates are still dead instead of holding the base line.
recordings.goodThing: At 19:52 you are in the better shape: allies are in front of Samira near mid, so you can damage from behind them instead of being the first body enemies reach.
recordings.whyTrust: The review is tied to the death-state frames: the game was already hard, so the fastest improvement is removing the extra deaths after the safe fight window is gone.
recordings.pattern: The repeated mistake is trying to solve a losing state by walking forward or staying in the fight. The higher-value habit is to switch from carry mode to damage-control mode the moment allies are dead, enemy tools are still available, or your HP no longer lets you survive a re-engage.
recordings.diamondRule: When the state is losing, your next click is not hero damage; it is safe wave, base line, or wait for bodies.
recordings.drill: Next game, say even or losing before every mid-game fight; if the answer is losing, your only allowed clicks are back, tower wave, or wait.

recordings.title: Passive Level 1 Botlane (Remake Situation)
recordings.feedbackTitle: Passive Level 1 Botlane (Remake Situation)
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You did not contest early lane state or pressure for level 2 when the enemy botlane was present and your support was AFK, conceding full wave and lane control. Fix: Auto-pilot AFK waits lose you both pressure and tempo in every game; instead, if your support is absent, focus on maximizing safe XP soak without ceding full control, using tower cover to grab as much gold/XP as possible or threaten with your range, and be alert for remake with minimal risk.
recordings.gameDetail: At 01:05 in botlane, enemy Nautilus and Senna arrive first to lane while your Karma is AFK in fountain/base, as seen on minimap and remake bar. You remain far back, not contesting any last hits or threatening from bush/under-tower, which hands complete wave control to Nautilus/Senna and will snowball lane state against you in any non-remake scenario. Level 1 Samira is weak, but leaving two champions uncontested guarantees they get prio, crash, and zone you—especially critical versus a ranged + engaged support where staying in XP range with minimal risk is mandatory. Challenger-level botlaners maximize whatever XP, single last hits, and bush vision they can grab even in a lost 2v1, because ceding 1-2 minutes of lane XP is unrecoverable. Around 1:04, at and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested; By 1:16, at and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested. Even in remake games, build the discipline to claim auto-range last hits and punish enemy over-aggression under turret to prevent permanent low-tempo habits from forming.
recordings.secondaryFocus: Second focus: Lane posturing and A-click/camera discipline—your camera and positioning snap to max distance from Nautilus/Senna with zero check of their spellcast animations or fog threat; in future games, hold camera at spell range and A-click toward melee minions just out of hook range to pressure every possible XP safely, even at low FPS.
recordings.eventEvidence: 1:04 At and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested; 1:16 At and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested
recordings.goodThing: You do not greed forward or die to a likely Nautilus hook/trap in a doomed 1v2.
recordings.whyTrust: In Challenger bot lanes, players denied from level 2 lose all ability to control crash, XP, and safe farm for 5+ minutes; claiming wave XP and exerting even minimal threat is the only way to prevent snowball disasters.
recordings.evidence: 1:04 At and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested; 1:16 At and , you hover well behind your minion wave while Nautilus/Senna step up uncontested
recordings.pattern: The main issue is complete surrender of lane presence in AFK support/remake scenarios, with no attempt to maximize XP or contest single last hits. Camera stays far back with no spell-range active threat checks, and no defensive posture for recoverable XP.
recordings.diamondRule: Never surrender full wave XP and pressure without at least testing safe last hits or auto-range trade windows, especially versus engage supports—every uncontested push doubles the lane bleed.
recordings.drill: Next time support is AFK or lane is lost, hold tower and auto-range for as many minions/XP ticks as safely possible, using camera toggle and A-click to check for engages.

recordings.title: Failed to Rotate Off Sidelane to Pressure Mid
recordings.feedbackTitle: Failed to Rotate Off Sidelane to Pressure Mid
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You stayed bot and continued farming long after the bot turret fell and your team grouped for mid/inhib plays. Fix: After first turret and the wave is past river, you must rotate to mid to pressure with your team and threaten objectives instead of leaking time farming bot alone.
recordings.gameDetail: After 13:16 (frame 6) you and your enchanter crash down the last hits on bot outer with all dragon-side objectives cleared and enemy pressure breaking, but you stay bot side for multiple minutes in the following frames, chasing bot wave and jungle instead of rotating mid where your teammates group for a heavy push (frame 10, 24:28 clock visible). This indecision means you arrive late to mid plays and miss the highest-value window to threaten inner turrets or help your team snowball with numbers—especially risky because Samira’s mid-game threat is highest when grouped. Sticking to a dead lane after tower is down delays your tempo advantage, risks unnecessary 1vX catches, and stalls snowball; the higher-level payoff is collapsing mid, using pushed bot to force rotates, and fighting around your team or objective. That route is better because mid is shortest for rotations, connects with teammates for both safety and max pressure, and applies the kind of tempo punishment Master players expect.
recordings.secondaryFocus: Second focus: Fix late recalls and wasted time low in lane—when low HP and after fighting or objective, recall immediately rather than half-heartedly farming dead waves; next game, recall as soon as your productive play is over or you’re at risk.
recordings.eventEvidence: 13:15 From onward, bot turret is gone, wave is pushed, team groups mid, but Samira is caught farming bot and hovering alone with no dragon or tower to take. 21:21 Samira in the middle of a recall at outer bot turret, ally lane minions and enemies nearby. 24:28 After (frame 6) you and your enchanter crash down the last hits on bot outer with all dragon-side objectives cleared and enemy pressure breaking, but you stay bot side for multiple minutes in the following frames, chasing bot wave and jungle instead of rotating mid where your teammates group for a heavy push (frame 10, clock visible).
recordings.goodThing: You correctly converted early bot tower pressure with support backup and did not greed-dive post-tower, showing some conversion discipline.
recordings.whyTrust: This feedback tracks with visible team groupings, minimap rotations, and open structures: not rotating to mid means you miss the team snowball timing and risk solo deaths when you are needed to punch through mid or defend; this is a repeatable core ADC habit at all ranks.
recordings.evidence: 13:15 From onward, bot turret is gone, wave is pushed, team groups mid, but Samira is caught farming bot and hovering alone with no dragon or tower to take. 21:21 Samira in the middle of a recall at outer bot turret, ally lane minions and enemies nearby. 24:28 After (frame 6) you and your enchanter crash down the last hits on bot outer with all dragon-side objectives cleared and enemy pressure breaking, but you stay bot side for multiple minutes in the following frames, chasing bot wave and jungle instead of rotating mid where your teammates group for a heavy push (frame 10, clock visible).
recordings.pattern: After securing bot tower with help, you stay bot side and farm past lane expiration, instead of joining team for mid pressure and conversion. This leaks your threat window and delays your team’s ability to break open the map.
recordings.diamondRule: Never stay in dead lane solo after tower when teammates are grouping mid—rotate through mid and show for team pressure or objectives.
recordings.drill: In your next 3 games, after taking bot outer, push wave past river then hard-rotate mid at the next recall or lane reset and group for at least two mid waves and one turret push.

recordings.title: Spend the base win before the next fight
recordings.feedbackTitle: Spend the base win before the next fight
recordings.feedback: Mistake: after turning the lead into enemy-base pressure, you kept the next fight window alive while carrying a large unspent shutdown. Fix: after the base kill or tower damage, reset unless nexus is immediately finishable.
recordings.gameDetail: At 1:31 you are already farming bot safely with the wave in front, and by 3:23 you take the first bot-side fight instead of only bleeding CS; that early pressure is good. At 4:36 you correctly turn the lane win into bot tower damage with allies and minions, and at 6:56 you are still converting through bot-side structure pressure. The leak starts after the map is already cracked: at 9:47 you are walking near the enemy base with multiple enemies alive, and at 10:36 you get another kill inside the base while holding about 2497 gold. That fight works in beginner bots, but the Master-climb reason to reset is simple: unspent gold is not real power yet, and giving a shutdown before buying would erase the tempo you already earned. At 12:21 you are back on the map after spending, which is the cleaner version; the repeatable rule is base damage or a base kill first, then spend the gold unless the nexus is free right now.
recordings.eventEvidence: 1:31 shows Samira safely farming bot with wave control. 4:36 shows Samira hitting bot tower with allies and minions. 9:47 shows Samira near the enemy base while enemies are still alive. 10:36 shows a base kill while Samira is holding about 2497 gold. 12:21 shows Samira back on the map after spending.
recordings.goodThing: You did convert the lane lead into tower/base pressure instead of only chasing kills.

recordings.pattern: The improvement is real: you are finding waves, towers, and the base. The next leak is staying available for one more base fight after the payout while your gold is still unspent.
recordings.diamondRule: After a base kill or structure payout, reset with big gold unless the nexus is free on the current wave.
recordings.drill: Next game, when your gold is above 2000 after a base fight, say 'nexus or reset' before clicking forward.

recordings.title: Turn the shutdown into a grouped push
recordings.feedbackTitle: Turn the shutdown into a grouped push
recordings.feedback: Mistake: after spending a huge lead, you let side fights test your shutdown before your team was set up to take tower or base. Fix: choose the safest visible payout first: group with the wave and allies, hit free structure, or reset if teammates cannot follow.
recordings.gameDetail: At 09:11 you are in base/shop after a strong 4/0/2 start, and that part is good because you spend instead of dragging raw gold around. At 10:25 you are 6/0/2 with 1285 gold in a bot-side lane fight right after a shutdown, so the leak is testing the fed Samira shutdown in a side lane before the team is organized around a guaranteed tower, objective setup, or reset. At 11:12 the better shape appears: you are grouped mid behind allies with enemies in front; mid is better here because allies are already there, the lane points to towers/base, and enemies must defend structure instead of collapsing from fog. At 12:42 you are 8/0/2 mid while several allies are dead or on timers, so the next check is wait, reset, or hold the wave until the next push is together, because one more solo wave does not matter if it gives away your shutdown. By 13:10 and 13:24 you do enter base with the team, which proves the ending instinct is there; make the earlier 10:25 and 12:42 windows look like that team-linked ending sooner.
recordings.eventEvidence: 09:11 shows Samira in shop after a 4/0/2 start with Immortal Shieldbow visible and low remaining gold. 10:25 shows Samira 6/0/2 in bot-side lane during a shutdown message with enemies and a wave still present. 11:12 shows Samira grouped mid behind allies while enemies are in front. 12:42 shows Samira 8/0/2 mid while multiple allies are dead or on timers. 13:10 to 13:24 shows the team turning the lead into enemy-base damage.
recordings.goodThing: You did spend, you did group with allies, and you did end through the base. The improvement is protecting the shutdown lead between those good moments.
recordings.whyTrust: The frames show both the good habit and the leak: you bought at 09:11 and ended with the team at 13:24, but the risky windows were the side-fight exposure at 10:25 and the alone-before-team state at 12:42.
recordings.evidence: Manual frame review of the May 21 queued auto capture: shop/spend at 09:11, side-fight shutdown state at 10:25, grouped mid pressure at 11:12, unsynced mid wave with ally death timers at 12:42, and base conversion at 13:10-13:24.
recordings.pattern: The repeated climb leak is letting a won personal lead pass through extra side fights before the next team objective is organized. The fix is not always mid; it is whichever visible route turns the lead into structure, objective setup, or a clean reset with the least shutdown exposure.
recordings.diamondRule: After a big Samira spend, do not take a fair side fight until you have first checked the guaranteed payout: group, structure, objective, or reset.
recordings.drill: When you are 4/0 or better, say payout first before accepting the next side fight.

recordings.title: Low-HP Overstay After Wins
recordings.feedbackTitle: Low-HP Overstay After Wins
recordings.feedback: Mistake: After winning trades or securing kills, you stay too long with low HP and no clear next payout, risking enemy return and losing reset tempo. Fix: Recall and spend after a successful fight or turret unless there is a guaranteed safe tower hit or protected wave, especially when HP is low or enemy spawns are up.
recordings.gameDetail: Around 0:50, Samira is in the bot lane with the first minion wave, positioned close to her own tower, uncontested by enemy champions; By 1:29, clear evidence for the overstaying lesson: Samira is low HP after winning a fight and lingers in lane instead of recalling to reset and spend gold, showing the risk of enemy return and loss of tempo; Then around 3:06, Samira is mid-fight in bot lane, shielded and actively auto-attacking an enemy amid a large minion wave. This matters because a Samira lead only climbs when the next click protects the shutdown and turns the won moment into a map result before enemies get another collapse window.

recordings.goodThing: You correctly push for tower when it is free (02:31-02:46), converting the win to structure.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira with a high gold bank gets far more aggressive power and survival from immediate purchases; Master players abuse slow resets for easy kills and map swaps.
recordings.evidence: 0:50 Samira is in the bot lane with the first minion wave, positioned close to her own tower, uncontested by enemy champions. 1:29 Clear evidence for the overstaying lesson: Samira is low HP after winning a fight and lingers in lane instead of recalling to reset and spend gold, showing the risk of enemy return and loss of tempo. 3:06 Samira is mid-fight in bot lane, shielded and actively auto-attacking an enemy amid a large minion wave.
recordings.pattern: When ahead, Alan chases more waves or jungle after tower and fight wins, delaying his buy and re-entry and exposing himself to enemy respawns or collapses.
recordings.diamondRule: When you finish a fight or take a tower and have over 1000g or 30% HP, reset immediately unless the next structure is directly free and unguarded.
recordings.drill: Next game, recall immediately after your first tower or big fight win unless there is a clear tower hit or guaranteed safe map play.

recordings.title: Base break became a delayed sync
recordings.feedbackTitle: Base break became a delayed sync
recordings.feedback: Mistake: after the base was cracked, you let the next minute become mid-wave cleanup and a late rejoin instead of an immediate finish-or-reset call. Fix: once the inhibitor/nexus area opens, say finish, reset, or sync next wave before touching another mid wave.
recordings.gameDetail: At 11:12 you and your team are already inside the enemy base with the structure line cracked, so the objective habit is real and that part is good. At 11:27 you correctly fight with allies in the base and get the takedown instead of chasing somewhere useless. At 11:47 the next decision point appears: the tower is down, you have about 3034 gold, minions and allies are still in the base, and the clean Master-climb call is either finish now or reset on the same timing as the team. At 12:32 and 12:42 you are back mid collecting waves after spending, which means the old reset critique was too vague; the actual leak is that the next base hit is no longer clearly synced. By 12:52 your team is fighting again near the opened base while you are still arriving from mid, and at 13:00 the rejoin works in this game, but stronger teams use that delay to clear waves, respawn, or collapse on the late carry. The lesson is: after base breaks, do not let a correct reset turn into a disconnected re-entry.
recordings.eventEvidence: 11:12 shows the base already cracked with Samira and allies inside; 11:27 shows the base fight working with allies; 11:47 shows about 3034 gold and a fresh tower-down window; 12:32 to 12:42 shows mid-wave cleanup after spending; 12:52 to 13:00 shows the late rejoin into the next base fight.
recordings.goodThing: You did create real base pressure with the team and appear to spend before the next mid wave; the fix is the re-entry timing after that, not pretending the whole sequence was bad.
recordings.whyTrust: The evidence changes the diagnosis: visible gold drops after the base break, so the mistake is not simply refusing to spend; it is losing team sync on the next push.
recordings.pattern: The repeated climb leak is not only chasing; it is letting a won base state turn into an unclear next state. Once the base is open, every next action should preserve finish pressure, reset value, or team sync.
recordings.diamondRule: After a base break, the next wave only matters if it syncs the next base hit.
recordings.drill: After inhibitor/base pressure, call one word before the next click: finish, reset, or sync.

recordings.title: Base win becomes side chase
recordings.feedbackTitle: Base win becomes side chase
recordings.feedback: Mistake: after the base was cracked, you kept accepting second and third fights away from the cleanest payout. Fix: after inhibitor/base pressure, call the state out loud: free structure, body blocking structure, or reset; only fight if it directly protects one of those.
recordings.gameDetail: At 20:58 you are already in the enemy base with an open lane and enough damage to keep the push moving; that part is good objective pressure, not the mistake. At 21:14 the fight has paid you, two allies are dead, and you are holding about 2700 unspent gold, so the next Master-climb decision is to reset or regroup instead of staying available for the next collapse. At 22:27 you run down bot side alone with the base still cracked, which leaks the map win into travel time and gives enemies another respawn window. The clearest mistake is the 27:08 to 27:12 chase into the side jungle after the base is already open; by 27:56 you are back in the base brawl at low HP with multiple defenders alive, which is exactly how a won beginner-bot base sequence becomes a shutdown or stall in harder games. The lesson is not stop fighting forever; it is stop fighting unless the fight removes the body between you and the structure.
recordings.eventEvidence: 20:58 Samira is in the enemy base hitting through minions and defenders. 21:14 shows the won fight plus about 2700 unspent gold. 22:27 shows a solo bot-side drift after base pressure. 27:08 to 27:12 shows the chase away from structures. 27:56 shows the low-HP re-entry into multiple defenders.
recordings.goodThing: You repeatedly found the base and created real ending pressure; the mistake is what happened after the first payout, not the fact that you went to objectives.
recordings.whyTrust: The evidence separates the good habit from the leak: you did get to the enemy base, but the later side chase and low-HP re-entry are the parts that stop the same lead from converting cleanly against stronger players.
recordings.evidence: Manual frame review of the queued May 21 full-game clip: visible base pressure at 20:58 and 21:14, solo bot-side drift at 22:27, side-jungle chase at 27:08-27:12, and low-HP base re-entry at 27:56.
recordings.pattern: The recurring leak is post-win state switching. You win enough of the first fight, then spend the next seconds on a side chase, extra body, or low-HP re-entry instead of choosing reset, regroup, or structure.
recordings.diamondRule: After a base win, every click must be one of three things: hit free structure, kill the body blocking structure, or reset with the gold.
recordings.drill: Next game, after inhibitor/base pressure, say 'free, blocked, or reset' before every forward click for the next 20 seconds.

recordings.title: Finish-window all-in
recordings.feedbackTitle: Finish-window all-in
recordings.feedback: Mistake: over-learning this clip would make you throw; the same dash is bad if it starts without wave, teammate body, or nexus pressure. Fix: full send only when every reset moves toward the payout.
recordings.gameDetail: At 14:21 the fight is already in their base, not in river or a side lane: allies and minions are in front, enemy defenders are packed near nexus, and Samira is close enough to chain resets without walking into fog. At 14:27 the first two kills land and the play becomes a real cleanup instead of a random chase. At 14:33 the chain reaches triple, at 14:38 the quadra happens beside the nexus area, and at 14:41 the penta lands because the fight never leaves the ending window. Learn this exactly: the all-in is good when the map is already ending; outside that condition, cash out first.
recordings.eventEvidence: 14:21 fight starts inside the enemy base with multiple enemy defenders still alive; 14:27 double kill; 14:33 triple; 14:38 quadra near nexus; 14:41 penta as the base remains open.
recordings.goodThing: The strong part is the finish discipline: once the enemy line collapses, Samira stays with the ally/minion push and cleans through the defenders instead of drifting sideways.
recordings.whyTrust: The penta is not random: the clip shows the conditions that made it valid, with allies and minions already in base and every reset moving toward nexus.
recordings.evidence: Manual storyboard review of the May 20 highlight: visible base fight from 14:21 to 14:41, double/triple/quadra/penta sequence, and enemy base already open.
recordings.pattern: Samira's best-looking fight here works because the map already gives the exit and the payout. The dangerous habit would be copying the same depth when the fight is not already a base finish.
recordings.diamondRule: All-in only when the reset path and the payout are the same direction.
recordings.drill: Before E/R, ask: does every reset move me toward nexus, tower, dragon, Baron, or safety?

recordings.feedbackTitle: You stay when one hit kills you
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you keep playing the wave when one auto or spell kills you. Fix: give the wave and recall before the lane turns into a death timer.
recordings.gameDetail: Honest read: this is not a mechanics issue; it is a health-gate issue. At 2:43 Samira is under bot tower with the enemy wave arriving, Ashe still visible to the right, and enough missing HP that the lane should already be treated as dangerous instead of playable. At 3:27 the real mistake is clearer: Samira is still beside the bot wave at lethal health, the camera and movement are staying with minions, and the recall path has not been taken even though one clean enemy touch can turn the wave into a death timer. At 4:57 the good version is visible after the reset: Samira is back on the map with enough HP to walk forward, Q/auto from range, and choose the next trade instead of begging not to be hit. By 14:28 the team is ending with Samira alive around the base fight, which proves the champion damage is not the blocker. The blocker is staying in lane after the health bar says the next decision should be recall.
recordings.eventEvidence: 2:43 Samira is under bot tower with Ashe still visible and the wave arriving; 3:27 Samira is still in lane at lethal health; 4:57 reset gives enough HP to re-enter; 14:28 the later team ending works.
recordings.goodThing: After the bad early health decision, you still got back into the game and converted later pressure into the ending push.
recordings.whyTrust: This is not vague advice: the reviewed frames show the exact one-hit-health lane stay, and removing that habit protects the aggression that already works later.
recordings.evidence: Manual storyboard review of the May 18 full recording: early lane death at lethal HP, later kills and turret/base conversion after grouping.
recordings.pattern: The new game is better at converting once Samira has items, but the early lane still has a lethal-HP greed point: staying for the wave while one hit from death turns a manageable recall into a death timer.
recordings.diamondRule: Below one enemy auto or spell, the wave is no longer the objective; reset first, then play the next item spike.
recordings.drill: At low HP, say one hit kills me and recall unless the enemy bot lane is dead or fully gone.

recordings.feedback: Mistake: You overstayed in enemy territory after taking inhibitor or nexus structures, instead of resetting safely with your team. Fix: After a structure take or inhib, immediately recall or group for the next cross-map play instead of staying for extra jungle camps or fights in the enemy base.
recordings.gameDetail: Around 7:22, blue team destroyed the first turret, Kayle and Samira mid lane; By 8:19, shutdown message in mid lane, Samira moving alone. This matters because a Samira lead only climbs when the next click protects the shutdown and turns the won moment into a map result before enemies get another collapse window.
recordings.eventEvidence: 7:22 Blue team destroyed the first turret, Kayle and Samira mid lane; 8:19 Shutdown message in mid lane, Samira moving alone
recordings.goodThing: You did convert a winning push into an inhibitor take before overstaying.
recordings.whyTrust: Resetting after objective takes is a fundamental Samira and teamfight carry habit that prevents int deaths, giving your team a clear map state to set up the next play safely.
recordings.evidence: 7:22 Blue team destroyed the first turret, Kayle and Samira mid lane; 8:19 Shutdown message in mid lane, Samira moving alone
recordings.pattern: Alan often converts fights or pushes into inhib takes correctly but leaks shutdown gold or control by sitting in exposed base territory after the play, risking catch or lost tempo.
recordings.diamondRule: Always reset or disengage after you take an inhibitor or nexus objective—never linger for extra camps or fights when the exit is free.
recordings.drill: After every inhibitor or nexus take in your next game, always call for recall or reset immediately; do not click forward or sideways until your team spends gold.

recordings.title: Ignored Nexus For Extra Kills
recordings.feedbackTitle: Ignored Nexus For Extra Kills
recordings.feedback: Mistake: After acing the enemy with Nexus exposed, you chased kills near fountain instead of immediately ending the game. Fix: Hit the exposed Nexus right away after the ace instead of chasing bonus kills.
recordings.gameDetail: At 16:29 the Nexus turrets are down and the cleanest next click is the Nexus. You get another kill, but then get crowd-controlled and die near fountain, leaving the Nexus unhit and creating a stall window. The lesson is simple: after an ace with Nexus exposed, stop fighting champions unless they are literally blocking the Nexus.
recordings.eventEvidence: 16:29 Nexus turrets down, structure window: Samira has open access to end by hitting the Nexus but instead starts chasing kills, directly supporting the coaching claim about ignoring a free structure.
recordings.goodThing: Played frontline cleanup very well and secured a multi-kill while focusing remaining threats before the ace.
recordings.whyTrust: Proven win-closing consistency: ending on the open Nexus as soon as possible denies enemy stall, fountain regeneration, and any respawn pick risk—especially crucial on a snowball finisher like Samira.
recordings.evidence: 16:29 Nexus turrets down, structure conversion window: Alan has open access to end by hitting the Nexus but instead starts chasing kills, directly supporting the coaching claim about ignoring a free structure for pad kills.
recordings.pattern: Alan gets caught up in post-ace fighting and pad-kill chasing, creating tempo and risk leak when the win is guaranteed by simply hitting Nexus. This habit risks giving up unnecessary shutdowns and throw scenarios.
recordings.diamondRule: Hit the exposed Nexus first after an ace—do not chase for fountain kills with the win opened.
recordings.drill: In your next game, if the Nexus is exposed and the enemy is aced, click and attack the Nexus immediately before considering any further aggression.

recordings.title: Low-health overstay after successful fight
recordings.feedbackTitle: Low-health overstay after successful fight
recordings.feedback: Mistake: Stayed in lane with very low HP after the fight when enemies with global spells or fast respawns could punish. Fix: Immediately recall or back up to safety after a successful fight if you're below 20% HP, especially when multiple enemy respawns are off the map.
recordings.gameDetail: This leaves an unnecessary window for respawned enemies or globals to collapse or snipe, especially with fogged Heimer/Kayle missing from the minimap. The correct next click is to start a recall behind your team after the final kill, especially when all spells are down and your HP cannot tank tower or new spells. Around 8:47, Samira and Shen move toward enemy Kindred under turret as ocean buff spawns; By 9:19, Samira remains at low HP under tower after fight instead of recalling; evidence of dangerous overstay after reset window opens. The lesson: converting a won fight with a low-health reset secures the lead instead of leaking free shutdown bounty or tempo.
recordings.eventEvidence: 8:47 Samira and Shen move toward enemy Kindred under turret as ocean buff spawns. 9:19 Evidence: Samira is visibly low HP and remains under tower after the fight, illustrating the mistake of overstaying instead of resetting. This frame marks the start of the punishable reset window where enemies could respawn and threaten her, which is exactly the lesson's focus.
recordings.goodThing: Joined the fight on time and played front-to-back, focusing appropriate targets for safe execution.
recordings.whyTrust: Almost every shutdown throw in ADC/Skirmisher games at your level comes from staying for one more wave, objective, or hit with no health, making this the highest-value change for solo queue consistency.
recordings.evidence: 8:47 Samira and Shen move toward enemy Kindred under turret as ocean buff spawns. 9:19 Evidence: Samira is visibly low HP and remains under tower after the fight, illustrating the mistake of overstaying instead of resetting. This frame marks the start of the punishable reset window where enemies could respawn and threaten her, which is exactly the lesson's focus.
recordings.pattern: You played the fight correctly but did not immediately leave or recall when critically low, risking a shutdown from respawns or long-range spells.
recordings.diamondRule: Never stay on the map with less than 25% HP if the enemy death timers are under 10 seconds and objectives are not instantly free—always reset to guarantee tempo and safety.
recordings.drill: After every major fight, check your HP bar: if it's red or under 25%, instantly press B unless you see a completely free structure or the next wave is still multiple seconds away.

recordings.feedbackTitle: Overforcing Into the Base Versus Grouped Defenders
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You forced into the enemy base versus multiple grouped defenders instead of playing for reset or structure. Fix: When you secure a kill and see grouped enemies on turret, back up, reset vision, or threaten structures instead of diving deep.
recordings.gameDetail: This puts you directly in front of both Anivia wall and Kayle, while enemy respawns are close and your team is split on the map. As a result, you get collapsed on and killed under their inhibitor (12:40), throwing away a shutdown and losing map tempo. When you get a pick with no minion wave on inhib, the lesson is to either push next structure with the wave or back out and reset vision, not force dives into grouped defenders.
recordings.eventEvidence: 12:40 Evidence of overforcing: this frame shows the shutdown and dive into grouped defenders in the enemy base, confirming the mistake of not resetting or threatening structure after a kill. 12:44 Fight at enemy base, Leona and other champions are clashing.
recordings.goodThing: You immediately converted on Gragas being out of position, securing the pick cleanly.
recordings.whyTrust: This specific mistake is exploitable by mass CC and respawn timers on any ADC, especially Samira who relies on resets, so following the advice avoids needless shutdowns and lost momentum.
recordings.evidence: 12:40 Evidence of overforcing: this frame shows the shutdown and dive into grouped defenders in the enemy base, confirming the mistake of not resetting or threatening structure after a kill. 12:44 Fight at enemy base, Leona and other champions are clashing.
recordings.pattern: Whenever you get a pick near their base, you chase deep for more kills instead of playing off wave or reset; this opens you to collapses by grouped or respawning enemies.
recordings.diamondRule: After a successful pick, always check for next wave or free turret before diving past inhibitor, especially when defenders are grouped or respawns are imminent.
recordings.drill: Next time you get a pick by the enemy base, immediately decide: is there a structured objective to hit or should you reset, no deep chases unless wave and numbers are guaranteed.

recordings.feedbackTitle: Stayed for low-HP 1v2 under tower after kill
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You stayed alone at low health and attempted a close-range 1v2 after getting a kill under enemy tower. Fix: After securing the tower dive kill, step back immediately to avoid dying to the next Samira or tower damage.
recordings.gameDetail: After diving and killing Anivia under the enemy tower, Samira remained in range for several seconds while low on health, leading to an unnecessary 1v1 with Swain and picking up a shutdown. Once the first kill was secured, there was a visible window to walk back safely, but the decision to stay and fight instead risked a death for no objective gain. This sequence gave up any tempo advantage and a large shutdown right after a successful tower dive. Around 4:13, Samira under allied tower is hit by Gragas ability shield visual; By 4:17, Samira secures the dive kill under tower—the correct reset window with clear opportunity to escape; Then around 4:22, Samira stays to fight Swain while low HP, showing the mistake of overstaying for a risky 1v2 instead of resetting. The lesson is simple: once you win the initial dive or kill, immediately reset your spacing and back out unless there are no immediate threats.
recordings.eventEvidence: 4:13 Samira under allied tower is hit by Gragas ability shield visual; 4:17 Evidence of the mistake window: Samira secures the kill under tower and has a safe opportunity to reset, but instead chooses to stay, which begins the overstaying and risky 1v2 mistake. 4:22 Samira fighting Swain and minions under blue botlane turret
recordings.goodThing: Good use of aggression to secure the first kill under turret.
recordings.whyTrust: Backing out after winning a kill under tower will keep your bounty safe, deny the enemy shutdown gold, and let you repeat plays with your lead; this is visible on both Samira's low health and the imminent threat on the map.
recordings.evidence: 4:13 Samira under allied tower is hit by Gragas ability shield visual; 4:17 Evidence of the mistake window: Samira secures the kill under tower and has a safe opportunity to reset, but instead chooses to stay, which begins the overstaying and risky 1v2 mistake. 4:22 Samira fighting Swain and minions under blue botlane turret
recordings.pattern: Alan consistently executes mechanically on dives but stays too long in post-fight danger windows, getting punished by the next rotated enemy or tower damage. The chase for extra kills exposes shutdowns and leaks tempo.
recordings.diamondRule: Always leave turret range and reset spacing immediately after a successful tower dive, unless your team is already collapsing for the next play.
recordings.drill: Next game, after every kill under tower, immediately back out to max range—even if there's another low enemy—unless your support is actively re-engaging.

recordings.feedbackTitle: Overstayed for solo kill, punished by post-fight turret death
recordings.feedback: Mistake: Chased deep for a solo kill under turret with low HP and no backup, resulting in being instantly killed by the respawning enemy. Fix: After the initial kill or escape, reset or retreat rather than greeting for another solo play under enemy threat.
recordings.gameDetail: After surviving the initial tower dive and 2v2 with Heimerdinger and Swain, Samira remained in the lane with low health and went back in for a kill on Heimerdinger just after the initial action (see 10:48). Despite getting the kill, Samira is left alone, visibly overextended, and is immediately picked off by the respawning Heimerdinger (10:53). The better next click would be to back off after the first survival, preserving shutdown and prepping for the next team play. A hard lesson: do not overstay for an unnecessary kill when escape is an option.
recordings.eventEvidence: 10:39 Samira fighting two enemy champions under allied turret with minions present; 10:44 Samira at low health clearing minions near allied turret; one enemy retreating; 10:48 Samira secures a risky solo kill at low HP instead of resetting, showing the overstay mistake
recordings.goodThing: Samira executed well in the duel and secured the initial kill cleanly.
recordings.whyTrust: This habit leaks games at every level on Samira, as giving over a shutdown or free death with no tower conversion ruins your tempo and opens the map for enemies.
recordings.evidence: 10:39 Samira fighting two enemy champions under allied turret with minions present; 10:44 Samira at low health clearing minions near allied turret; one enemy retreating; 10:48 Samira secures a risky solo kill at low HP instead of resetting, showing the overstay mistake
recordings.pattern: Samira stayed alone under enemy turret for a risky second kill instead of retreating, securing one, but got caught in low-health territory and died on the reset by the respawning enemy.
recordings.diamondRule: Never stay for a solo play under enemy turret after a risky fight unless your escape and enemy respawn timing are accounted for.
recordings.drill: Next game, if you get a kill low HP under enemy turret, immediately retreat toward safety or bush and recall—do not stay for another play.

Fix: Immediately reset, recall, or move defensively after taking the tower when you have no vision or support, instead of greedily pushing for extra waves.

Around 7:00, tower destroyed; this is the mandatory reset window—the correct play is to immediately recall or move away instead of overextending. The lesson is that securing the turret is a natural reset point, not an invite to overextend for one more wave with no cover.

recordings.evidence: 7:00 Tower destroyed; this is the mandatory reset window—the correct play is to immediately recall or move away instead of overextending.
recordings.pattern: Alan repeatedly stayed for another wave after a turret or fight win without team or vision, leading to solo deaths that hand over shutdown gold and leak all lane pressure.
recordings.diamondRule: After taking a turret, unless you have deep vision and numbers, reset or group—never solo push another wave beyond the old tower line.
recordings.drill: After every outer turret take, immediately plan your reset or rotate—do not stay alone for the next wave unless you can confirm enemy positions and have backup.

recordings.feedbackTitle: Low health overchase for 1v1 finisher
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You chased Heimerdinger for a kill with very low HP and no vision on potential enemy rotations. Fix: When you're this low and alone after a skirmish, immediately pull back and reset, not chase for the last hit.
recordings.gameDetail: At 4:39, Samira is left low HP in bot lane with a wave to clear, but instead of resetting safely, you approach and commit to an all-in against a fleeing Heimerdinger, despite lacking any HP buffer and having no backup or vision on possible enemy jungler/support rotations. The better move was to hard disengage after the push or use the window for a fast recall, denying the enemy a free trade or respawn punish. Trying to force this kill can backfire to respawn losses or even a cross-map play if you die here. Around 4:39, setup: Samira is low HP after a fight but chooses to stay in lane instead of resetting, setting up the risky overchase; By 4:46, consequence: Samira is still low HP but all-ins Heimerdinger instead of disengaging, showing the mistake of overchasing at 1-spell-from-death. The recurring lesson: When you're one spell from death, do not flip for the solo kill with no vision or map knowledge—reset the lane state and keep your gold/XP lead.
recordings.eventEvidence: 4:39 Samira stands back near wave, some minions attacking, post-fight. 4:46 Start of Samira's low-HP hard-commit all-in with no vision—this is the evidence of overchasing instead of disengaging or resetting.
recordings.goodThing: You aggressively traded to get Heimerdinger low and play for potential advantage.
recordings.whyTrust: This fix will protect your shutdown, let you keep tempo, and actually climb out of silver—Samira does not need to flip-close every low-HP 1v1 if the risk is losing map tempo, and every strong Samira resets here.
recordings.evidence: 4:39 Samira stands back near wave, some minions attacking, post-fight. 4:46 Start of Samira's low-HP hard-commit all-in with no vision—this is the evidence of overchasing instead of disengaging or resetting.
recordings.pattern: You stayed to chase a near-dead enemy with almost no HP, ignoring that any mistake or unseen enemy closes the trade and leaks your lead. In higher ranks, this always gets punished by map rotations or flash/ignite turns.
recordings.diamondRule: Never all-in at sub-25% HP in enemy territory when you have no support, vision, or respawn pressure—even if the kill looks free, resets win games.
recordings.drill: Next time you're this low after a trade and see a greedy chase window, hit B and leave, even if the kill looks right there.

recordings.title: Respect missing and incoming TPs
recordings.feedbackTitle: Respect missing and incoming TPs
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You stayed too far forward in lane and did not respect the enemy Teleport coming in while exposed. Fix: When your ADC is hitting the wave and the enemy TP is coming down, immediately back off from melee range and give ground until you confirm numbers and threat.
recordings.gameDetail: The wave was pushing forward with Caitlyn present and Samira in melee range of the enemy caster minions, with both Caitlyn and Samira pushing just past river. A Teleport appears directly into the lane, yet Samira does not back off; she is caught in the middle of the wave and immediately crowd-controlled and killed, giving a shutdown. The better response is to immediately pull back as soon as TP is channeled, especially when ahead and holding a shutdown, completely avoiding the play. Around 3:34, blue team pushes bottom lane with minions near enemy turret; By 3:37, red team Samira teleports in, blue Samira is stunned in bot lane fight; Then around 3:40, Samira is killed for a shutdown, showing the consequence of staying too far forward and not respecting the incoming Teleport. Always respect missing enemies and incoming Teleports when you are far up in lane.
recordings.eventEvidence: 3:34 Blue team pushes bottom lane with minions near enemy turret; 3:37 Red team Samira teleports in, blue Samira is stunned in bot lane fight; 3:40 Samira is killed for a shutdown—shows the consequence of staying too far forward and not respecting the incoming Teleport, supporting the lesson on respecting missing enemies and incoming TPs.
recordings.goodThing: You and your lane partner pushed the wave aggressively to pressure the enemy tower.
recordings.whyTrust: Getting caught by TP like this instantly swings tempo and gold back to the enemy—this is a punishable, avoidable error and safe habits here directly stop throwing leads.
recordings.evidence: 3:34 Blue team pushes bottom lane with minions near enemy turret; 3:37 Red team Samira teleports in, blue Samira is stunned in bot lane fight; 3:40 Samira is killed for a shutdown—shows the consequence of staying too far forward and not respecting the incoming Teleport, supporting the lesson on respecting missing enemies and incoming TPs.
recordings.pattern: Positioning aggressively in lane with a shutdown lead but not reacting to cross-map threats or Teleports results in immediate punish; the kill was 100% avoidable with minimal discipline.
recordings.diamondRule: If you see a Teleport coming in near you and you don't have numbers, back off instantly—never greed wave or aggression with shutdown gold exposed.
recordings.drill: In your next game, back away instantly on every enemy Teleport in your lane, even if you think they might not have kill pressure—build the habit of never greeting TP plays.

recordings.feedback: Mistake: you are dead as the red turret falls, so the objective win turns into a stagger instead of a clean reset. Fix: once the turret drops, exit with the wave or recall with the team before the next enemy bodies arrive.
recordings.gameDetail: The objective went through, so the good part is that the team got structure value; the leak is dying during or right after that conversion, which removes your ability to reset, spend, and help the next map play. The better decision after the turret falls is exit or recall, not staying in the lane long enough to become the stagger. The lesson is to make the objective win end the sequence cleanly.
recordings.goodThing: Your team broke the inhibitor turret, creating an opportunity for a powerful map reset or further pressure.
recordings.whyTrust: This death costs Samira both tempo and agency on the map, but the correct reset after a turret break directly leads to more vision and better drag/baron prep in every rank.
recordings.pattern: The structure conversion happened, but the death means the sequence did not end cleanly; the next improvement is exiting after the payout.
recordings.diamondRule: When the turret falls, the sequence is over unless the next structure is free right now.
